samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state0">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state1">Detecting edges.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">State variable.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="next_state">Next state variable.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="rise_reg">Rising edge output register.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="down_reg">Falling edge output register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, if reset is active, the state is set to state0. Otherwise, the state is updated to next_state.
            If the state is state0, the module checks for edges in the input signal a. If a rising edge is detected, the rise_reg is set to 1, and the state is updated to state1. 
            If a falling edge is detected, the down_reg is set to 1, and the state is updated to state1. If no edge is detected, the module remains in state0.
            If the state is state1, the module checks if the input signal a has changed. If the input signal has changed, the module resets the rise_reg or down_reg accordingly and returns to state0. 
            If the input signal has not changed, the module remains in state1.
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>
